title: malus sylvestris subsp. pumila
intro: Malus sylvestris subsp. pumila, also known as the dwarf crab apple or wild crab apple, is a subspecies of the Malus sylvestris plant, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Malus sylvestris subsp. pumila include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as a herbal infusion, decoction, or tincture, and its bark, leaves, and fruit have been used in traditional herbal medicine for centuries.
